targetUsers: GTM sales operators managing account lists, enrichment, and PitchBook-style matching
Finance and banking analysts reconciling workbooks, exports, timecards, and variance evidence
Harness engineers converting messy workflow evidence into repeatable eval gates

notes: The profile stores only workbook shapes, categories, and header-level signals; raw private rows are not a repo artifact.
Most files are company research, classification, PitchBook, ParselyFi, or healthtech/JPM workflows.
Finance and ops files include timecards, timesheets, expense sheets, cost exports, and transaction exports with formula and layout concerns.

topCases.persona: Account executive or analyst building a company brief for outreach
Solo founder using chat as a lightweight CRM and research queue
Investment-banking candidate or finance team completing a 3-statement modeling test
Healthcare investor classifying a large conference or PitchBook export
Sales operations analyst preparing a PitchBook upload and match review
GTM analyst scoring target accounts against a reusable AMO rubric
Founder, investor, or BD lead capturing a fresh call note before it becomes a spreadsheet row
Ops or finance reviewer approving contractor time

architectureReview.rule: Research must describe the workflow, architecture gap, and existing capability fit before it proposes evals or code.

evalTrustLevels.meaning: Generated from traces or research; useful for discussion and advisory runs, not a merge gate.
Backed by captured sources or online consensus; can create scoped handoffs, still not blocking by default.
Credible sources disagree; advisory only, and the eval should check that disagreement is surfaced.
Reviewed or accepted for critical use; may become blocking when deterministic and safety-safe.

architectureFitChecks: Can existing tools, prompts, context builders, and Convex mutations already handle the case?
If not, is the missing piece a query, action, mutation, tool schema, validator, or UI review affordance?
What is the smallest implementation that proves the workflow without a new subsystem?
What old or proposed layer can be avoided because the existing artifact/job/lock path is enough?

rootCauseCategories.meaning: The agent acted on old state or missing refreshed context.
The model chose a tool that could not satisfy the workflow contract.
A write occurred without a current source read and version.
The server mutation allowed an unsafe or under-specified state change.
The output lacked source-backed evidence or cited the wrong evidence.
Instructions or context did not define the workflow sharply enough.
Scope, privacy, or role gating was wrong or underspecified.
The chosen model, budget, or slice policy was unfit for the task.
The human review or approval surface obscured the right decision.
The eval target itself is suspect and needs research/calibration.

Unblock benchmark promotion step spreadsheetbench-chart-visual-probe: SpreadsheetBench V2 rendered/VLM chart grading prerequisites are not proven.
Unblock benchmark promotion step docker-sandbox-probe: Docker/Harbor process isolation is not proven in this environment.
Resolve architecture budget review items or rerun with explicit handoff evidence before implementation.
Run skipped free-route-discovery once prerequisites are present: pass --live and set OPENROUTER_API_KEY to discover current free-auto candidates.
Run skipped professional-live-catalog once prerequisites are present: pass --live and set OPENROUTER_API_KEY to prove the professional catalog with the cheap champion route.
Run skipped chat-intake-live-runtime once prerequisites are present: pass --live and set OPENROUTER_API_KEY to run the chat-intake room runtime against a real route.

generatedEvalIdeas.title: GTM PitchBook match with evidence and no CRM clobber
generatedEvalIdeas.persona: Sales operations analyst preparing a PitchBook upload and match review
generatedEvalIdeas.goal: For each uploaded company, compare firm name, website, HQ location, and ticker against the matched result row; write match status, confidence, and short evidence without overwriting source CRM fields.

existingCapabilityNotes: Artifact refs, CellPayload evidence, lock/CAS, and workflow eval scaffolds already exist.
missingCapabilityNotes: Needs a concrete trace fixture before becoming a gate.
architectureFit.smallestChange: Add a deterministic fixture and assertions before touching runtime code.
requiredEvidence: Every new match/status cell is a CellPayload with at least one evidence item pointing to a source artifact and row.
Original firm name, website, HQ, country, and ticker cells are unchanged.

assertions.description: Every new match/status cell is a CellPayload with at least one evidence item pointing to a source artifact and row.
Original firm name, website, HQ, country, and ticker cells are unchanged.
Ambiguous duplicate-name rows are marked needs_review instead of guessed.

generatedEvalIdeas.title: Finance variance reconciliation with formula-safe writes
generatedEvalIdeas.goal: Aggregate spend by date and model/vendor, compare against expected totals, write variance and note cells with evidence, and skip already-correct cells.

existingCapabilityNotes: Formula dependency locks, CellPayload evidence, and no-clobber paths are already part of the harness.
missingCapabilityNotes: Needs one concrete variance fixture with formula-preservation assertions.

mutations: CAS write only changed variance notes

architectureFit.smallestChange: Add an eval fixture and validator around existing spreadsheet tools before adding any new tool.
requiredEvidence: Already-correct cells are skipped without version bumps.
Incorrect cells are corrected only after a current read and CAS-protected write.

assertions.description: Already-correct cells are skipped without version bumps.
Incorrect cells are corrected only after a current read and CAS-protected write.
Formula children are locked or flagged when parent input cells are edited.

generatedEvalIdeas.title: Eval harness generated from workflow rubrics
generatedEvalIdeas.persona: Harness engineer converting real workflows into regression tests
generatedEvalIdeas.goal: Read the eval template rows, generate NodeRoom run cases with persona, preconditions, expected artifact state, trace assertions, and cost/runtime budgets.

existingCapabilityNotes: agent:improve already produces local artifacts and deterministic gates.
missingCapabilityNotes: Research-derived evals need confidence and gate policy before they can block.
architectureFit.smallestChange: Keep this as an advisory eval fixture until the rubric is not contested.
requiredEvidence: Every eval has a starting state, task, allowed tools, expected state, expected trace, and budget.
Slow/free-auto cases are routed to /free and include checkpoint assertions.

contestedClaims: Different teams disagree on how much human review is required before eval promotion.

assertions.description: Every eval has a starting state, task, allowed tools, expected state, expected trace, and budget.
Slow/free-auto cases are routed to /free and include checkpoint assertions.
Interactive cases stay on the ladder-proven fast model.

reasons: contested claims must stay advisory until resolved or explicitly modeled
demoted: architecture budget is red (forbidden surfaces dirty / review required) — human approval before any implementation handoff

architectureBudget.allowedScope: Only files or modules named by the failing trace, failing eval, or handoff evidence.
defaultAllowedAreas: src/agent runtime, tools, context, and compaction
Convex job/tool adapters that already participate in the failing flow
eval fixtures and deterministic assertions for the affected workflow
forbiddenWithoutHumanApproval: graph/wiki/embedding expansion without a failing workflow eval
weakened CAS, lock, draft, auth, privacy, or eval gates
